channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oburu Oginga Absent from ODM Retreat, Emphasises Unity and Fair Competition
item.description: ODM party leader Oburu Oginga was unable to attend the party retreat in Mombasa on Monday, May 4, which brought together the national executive council, council of governors, MPs, trustees, and other ...

item.title: Minors Trespass into NYC Scientology Building, Cause Damage and Injure Staff
item.description: On Saturday afternoon, a group of teenagers forced their way into a Church of Scientology building in New York City, located near Times Square. The NYPD reported the incident occurred at around 4:24 P...

item.title: Recent Earthquakes Highlight Nevada’s Seismic Risks Near Reno and Las Vegas
item.description: Nevada has experienced a series of recent earthquakes that, while not causing major damage, underscore the state’s hidden seismic risks. On April 13, a magnitude 5.7 earthquake struck 45 miles east of...

item.title: Live Updates on 2026 By-Election Results Across Seven Constituencies
item.description: The news article was intended to provide live updates on the 2026 by-election results for seven constituencies: Baramati, Rahuri, Bagalkot, Dharmanagar, Umreth, Koridang, and Davanagere South. However...

item.title: How decluttering before a move can save money and reduce stress
item.description: Moving house can be stressful and costly, but decluttering beforehand can save both time and money. Australian households are increasingly cluttered, with one in four reporting more items than five ye...

item.title: Swiss businessman Marc Rieben stands trial for the murder of his wife Kristina Joksimovic
item.description: A 43-year-old Swiss businessman named Marc Rieben has gone on trial in Muttenz, near Basel, accused of murdering his 38-year-old wife, Kristina Joksimovic, a former Miss Switzerland finalist, in Febru...

item.title: Assam Assembly Election Updates: BJP Leading, Himanta Biswa Sarma Ahead in Jalukbari, Gaurav Gogoi Loses in Jorhat
item.description: Counting of votes for the 2026 Assam Assembly elections is underway with significant leads reported in several constituencies. Assam Chief Minister and Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) leader Himanta Bisw...

item.title: P.E.I. oyster industry turns to U.S. disease-resistant seed to recover from mass die-off
item.description: Prince Edward Island’s oyster industry is attempting to recover from a severe crisis caused by two diseases, MSX and dermo, which have led to widespread oyster mortality across the province. In respon...

item.title: Bandits Kill 11 People In Retaliation Attack In Katsina Villages
item.description: On Sunday, for Kankia Local Government Area, Katsina State, no fewer than eleven people lost their lives after bandits carried out reprisal attacks on Jeka da Kolo and Kwalgoro villages. Police say th...

item.title: Ana Viladomiu: Living in Gaudí’s Casa Milà for Almost Four Decades
item.description: Ana Viladomiu, aged 70, is the final tenant of Antoni Gaudí’s Casa Milà, also known as La Pedrera, situated on the prestigious Passeig de Gràcia in Barcelona. She has resided in the building since 198...

item.title: IHC schedules main appeals of Imran Khan and Bushra Bibi in £190m case for May 7
item.description: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) has decided to hear the main appeals of PTI chairman Imran Khan and his wife Bushra Bibi in the £190 million Al-Qadir Trust case on May 7, 2026. The court dismissed thei...

item.title: Princess Eugenie and Jack Brooksbank announce third pregnancy
item.description: Buckingham Palace has confirmed that Princess Eugenie and her husband, Jack Brooksbank, are expecting their third child. The announcement noted that His Majesty The King has been informed and is thril...

item.title: Scientists Trace Leonardo da Vinci’s Lineage to Reconstruct His Genetic Profile
item.description: An international scientific initiative called the Leonardo DNA Project is making strides toward uncovering the genetic makeup of Leonardo da Vinci. Spanning over 30 years, researchers have traced da V...

item.title: Gauteng residents face infrastructure failures despite Freedom Day ideals
item.description: This reader’s letter argues that many Gauteng residents had little reason to celebrate Freedom Day due to the ongoing collapse of basic infrastructure across the province. Despite Gauteng being regard...

item.title: Gov Dauda Lawal Urges Air War College Participants to Turn Zamfara Study Tour Insights into Security Solutions
item.description: Governor Dauda Lawal of Zamfara State don call on participants of Air War Course 12 from the Air Force War College to take the lessons wey dem learn during their study tour of the state and use am to ...

item.title: UK set for cooler temperatures and scattered showers following bank holiday
item.description: Following last week's unseasonably warm conditions, the first full week of May in the UK is expected to see a noticeable drop in temperatures. While parts of England experienced highs of around 25°C (...

item.title: British tourist in critical condition in Johannesburg hospital after confirmed hantavirus case on Atlantic cruise ship
item.description: The South African Department of Health has confirmed that a British national is in critical condition in a Sandton private hospital in Johannesburg after contracting hantavirus during a cruise ship vo...

item.title: Government Plans to Reopen Kenya-Somalia Border to Formalise Trade
item.description: Health CS Aden Duale has expressed support for the reopening of the Kenya-Somalia border, highlighting the potential boost to trade and revenue collection. He pointed out that while the border has bee...

item.title: NDA Leads in Puducherry Assembly Election 2026 with Key Constituency Wins
item.description: The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is all set to retain power in Puducherry for a second consecutive term following the April 9, 2026 assembly elections. Voter turnout in the Union Territory was r...

item.title: Pakistan Navy conducts humanitarian rescue of Indian-led crew after vessel malfunction in Arabian Sea
item.description: The Pakistan Navy carried out a humanitarian rescue operation in the Arabian Sea after receiving an emergency request regarding a distressed merchant vessel, MV Gautam. According to security sources, ...
